data.narrative:
INERATEC is a Series B climate and energy company that has raised $129M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of six same-stage, same-sector private companies (Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc., among others), supplemented by one public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $129M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $245M. Peer-median analysis across the six-company climate and energy cohort yields a median valuation of $355M, with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B—the wide dispersion reflecting heterogeneous technology risk and market size within the sector. The peer-median result is materially higher than stage-progression, reflecting the composition of the comparison set, which includes high-valuation outliers like Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $208M (low) to $408M (high), with a median point estimate of $300M. Key uncertainties center on peer selection volatility—the $5.0B outlier meaningfully elevates the peer median—and INERATEC's position relative to peers on technology maturity, capital efficiency, and addressable market. The wide range suggests a valuation within the $250M–$350M band warrants further due diligence on unit economics and competitive positioning.
